title: ModuleInstance Object
description: Placed reference to a module on a schematic sheet.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: angle
properties.description: Rotation angle in degrees, counter-clockwise from the positive X axis.

properties.name: column
properties.description: Frame column position (1-based), or ? if outside a frame or frame locations are not set.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: mirror
properties.description: True if this instance is mirrored about the Y axis.

properties.name: module
properties.description: Module definition that this instance references.

properties.name: moduleVariant
properties.description: Name of the assembly variant applied to this instance; empty if using the default.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Assigned name of this module instance.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: offset
properties.description: Numeric offset added to part designators within this instance (e.g., R17 with offset 100 becomes R117).

properties.name: row
properties.description: Frame row position (A, B, C...), or ? if outside a frame or frame locations are not set.

properties.name: sheet
properties.description: Sheet number (1-based) where this instance is placed.

properties.name: x
properties.description: X coordinate of the placement origin, in internal units.

properties.name: y
properties.description: Y coordinate of the placement origin, in internal units.
